heading: General remedial measures for new plant pests and noxious weeds
label: 7714 General remedial measures for new plant pests and noxious weeds

children.heading: Authority to hold, treat, or destroy items
children.label: a Authority to hold, treat, or destroy items
A
children.text: the Secretary has reason to believe is a plant pest or noxious weed or is infested with a plant pest or noxious weed at the time of the movement; or

children.label: B
children.text: is or has been otherwise in violation of this chapter;
has not been maintained in compliance with a post-entry quarantine requirement; or

is the progeny of any plant, biological control organism, plant product, plant pest, or noxious weed that is moving into or through the United States or interstate, or has moved into the United States or interstate, in violation of this chapter.

children.heading: Authority to order an owner to treat or destroy
children.label: b Authority to order an owner to treat or destroy

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may order the owner of any plant, biological control organism, plant product, plant pest, noxious weed, article, or means of conveyance subject to action under subsection (a) to treat, apply other remedial measures to, destroy, or otherwise dispose of the plant, biological control organism, plant product, plant pest, noxious weed, article, or means of conveyance, without cost to the Federal Government and in the manner the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Failure to comply
children.label: 2 Failure to comply
children.text: If the owner fails to comply with the Secretary’s order under this subsection, the Secretary may take an action authorized by subsection (a) and recover from the owner the costs of any care, handling, application of remedial measures, or disposal incurred by the Secretary in connection with actions taken under subsection (a).

children.heading: Development required
children.label: 1 Development required
children.text: To facilitate control of noxious weeds, the Secretary may develop a classification system to describe the status and action levels for noxious weeds. The classification system may include the current geographic distribution, relative threat, and actions initiated to prevent introduction or distribution.

children.heading: Management plans
children.label: 2 Management plans
children.text: In conjunction with the classification system, the Secretary may develop integrated management plans for noxious weeds for the geographic region or ecological range where the noxious weed is found in the United States.

children.heading: Application of least drastic action
children.label: d Application of least drastic action
children.text: No plant, biological control organism, plant product, plant pest, noxious weed, article, or means of conveyance shall be destroyed, exported, or returned to the shipping point of origin, or ordered to be destroyed, exported, or returned to the shipping point of origin under this section unless, in the opinion of the Secretary, there is no less drastic action that is feasible and that would be adequate to prevent the dissemination of any plant pest or noxious weed new to or not known to be widely prevalent or distributed within and throughout the United States.
